_comment: Drop this into any Element 22 product repo as .claude/settings.json. When teammates trust the project folder, Claude Code, Claude Cowork, and Claude.ai (Chat) will prompt them to install the e22-plugins marketplace and connect GitHub. This file IS meant to be committed; it is per-project, not per-user. User-specific overrides go in .claude/settings.local.json which is gitignored.

enabledPlugins._comment_lanes: The two lane plugins. POs typically only need prototype-lane (Chat/Cowork). Engineers benefit from both — they receive handoffs into production-lane and may /propose directly.
enabledPlugins._comment_house_rules: The six house-rule plugins. These apply lane-aware rules to every Claude session (PO or Dev). In Claude Code they enforce via hooks; in Chat and Cowork they enforce via in-prompt rules + PR-level CI. The net effect is identical for anything that ships. Disable any individual one in .claude/settings.local.json if you need to opt out temporarily, but the team default is all six enabled.

_comment_connectors: The e22-plugins workflow REQUIRES the GitHub connector. Claude will prompt the user to connect on first use of any lane command. Recommended: Sentry, Statsig (accessed via the OpenFeature SDK for /promote), Microsoft Teams, context7. See CONNECTORS.md in the marketplace repo for the full reference. The keys below are documentary metadata, not validated settings.

notes.statsig: Accessed through the OpenFeature SDK so the flag platform stays swappable.
